description_long: This is a twin-engine, propeller-driven military airplane with a straight, high-mounted wing configuration and a distinctive twin-boom tail design. The fuselage is slender and streamlined, with a forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit that features a clear canopy. The engines are mounted on the wings, each with a large, four-bladed propeller. The tail section consists of two vertical stabilizers connected by a horizontal stabilizer, forming an H-tail. The exterior is primarily light gray with military insignia visible on the wings and fuselage. The landing gear is not visible, suggesting it may be retractable.
description: A light gray, twin-propeller military airplane with a high-mounted straight wing and twin-boom H-tail, featuring a forward enclosed cockpit and military markings.
description_view_0: Side view showing the twin-boom tail, high-mounted wings, and both propeller engines clearly.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the forward cockpit, both propellers, and the straight wing span.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, again showing the twin-boom tail and engine placement, with military insignia visible.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the H-tail configuration and the back of the engines and propellers.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing, straight configuration with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Twin turboprop engines mounted on the wings.
attribute_tail_design: H-tail configuration with twin vertical stabilizers connected by a horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Retractable landing gear (not visible in images).
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a clear canopy, likely multi-seat.
